mechanism: Inhibits multiple receptor tyrosine kinases with reported FLT3 activity
Broad kinase inhibition; reported activity in some FLT3-driven contexts
additional_notes: Activity noted in some FLT3-driven contexts

mechanism: Inhibits anti-apoptotic BCL-2 family proteins (BCL-2 and BCL-xL) to promote apoptosis
additional_notes: Also referred to as ABT-263; thrombocytopenia limits clinical use but useful preclinically

mechanism: Gossypol-derived compound with BH3 mimetic-like pro-apoptotic activity and multiple targets
additional_notes: Alternative name: gossypol; noted as multiple targets

mechanism: Inhibits MEK1/2 in the MAPK pathway to suppress proliferation in MAPK-active AML subsets
additional_notes: Described as effective in MAPK-active AML subsets

mechanism: Inhibits histone deacetylases to alter chromatin/transcription and reduce tumor cell viability
Potent HDAC inhibition leading to transcriptional changes and apoptosis
Weak HDAC inhibition with epigenetic effects; often requires high concentrations in vitro
additional_notes: Weak potency; frequently requires mM concentrations in vitro

mechanism: Inhibits the proteasome to induce proteotoxic stress and apoptosis
additional_notes: Cited with multiple AML studies; suggested positive control

mechanism: Irreversibly inhibits the proteasome to trigger proteostasis stress and apoptosis
Inhibits thioredoxin reductase to increase oxidative stress and promote cell death
additional_notes: Induces oxidative stress; activity reported in AML models

mechanism: Raises lysosomal pH and inhibits autophagy/lysosomal function, sometimes enhancing AML cell death
additional_notes: Modest as single agent; sometimes enhances AML cell death
